req.title: Card freeze / unfreeze
req.statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three primary test dimensions: (1) happy-path freeze/unfreeze toggling with state verification and authorization rejection (STC, EP), (2) non-existent card returning 404 (NEG), and (3) boundary transition where a card is frozen then immediately re-frozen (idempotency/boundary, BVA). A fifth test covers the full freeze→auth-reject→unfreeze→auth-pass cycle via a Decision Table. Together these give EP, BVA, STC, DT, and NEG coverage. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and clean up a fresh card, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_freeze_active_card_succeeds
preconditions: Card belongs to the caller and is in ACTIVE state
actions: Assert authorization network notified via mock/stub
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_active_card_succeeds(active_card, authed_client, mock_auth_network):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"
    mock_auth_network.assert_called_once_with(card_id, "frozen")

tests.name: test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404
preconditions: Card ID used does not exist in the system
expected: Response body contains error indicating card not found
trace: R-FIN-010: error pathway – 404 when card does not exist
tests.executable:
import pytest

NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID = "00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000"

def test_freeze_nonexistent_card_returns_404(authed_client):
    resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{NONEXISTENT_CARD_ID}/freeze")

    assert resp.status_code == 404
    body = resp.json()
    assert "not found" in body.get("error", "").lower() or "not found" in body.get("message", "").lower()

tests.name: test_freeze_already_frozen_card_is_idempotent
preconditions: Card belongs to the caller and is already in FROZEN state
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze a second time on an already-frozen card
Assert response indicates frozen state without error
expected: No duplicate auth-network notifications beyond the first freeze
trace: R-FIN-010: card state updated (boundary – freeze at already-frozen boundary)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_already_frozen_card_is_idempotent(frozen_card, authed_client, mock_auth_network):
    card_id = frozen_card["id"]

    resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")

    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "frozen"
    # idempotent: notifier must not fire a redundant event
    mock_auth_network.assert_not_called()

tests.name: test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt
preconditions: Card belongs to the caller and transitions from ACTIVE → FROZEN
actions: POST /cards/{id}/freeze to transition card to FROZEN
expected: Freeze returns HTTP 200 with status frozen
Authorization attempt returns HTTP 402 or body with declined status
trace: R-FIN-010: frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts
R-FIN-010: card state updated (ACTIVE → FROZEN transition)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_frozen_card_rejects_authorization_attempt(active_card, authed_client, mock_auth_network):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    freeze_resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    auth_resp = <simulate_authorization>(authed_client, card_id)
    assert auth_resp.status_code in (402, 200)
    assert auth_resp.json().get("decision") == "declined"

tests.name: test_freeze_then_unfreeze_restores_active_state
preconditions: Card belongs to the caller and is in ACTIVE state
actions: Assert auth-network notified for each state change
expected: Auth-network notifier called twice: once per state change
trace: R-FIN-010: card state updated (ACTIVE → FROZEN → ACTIVE)
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_freeze_then_unfreeze_restores_active_state(active_card, authed_client, mock_auth_network):
    card_id = active_card["id"]

    freeze_resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/freeze")
    assert freeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert freeze_resp.json()["status"] == "frozen"

    unfreeze_resp = authed_client.post(f"/cards/{card_id}/unfreeze")
    assert unfreeze_resp.status_code == 200
    assert unfreeze_resp.json()["status"] == "active"

    assert mock_auth_network.call_count == 2
    calls = mock_auth_network.call_args_list
    assert calls[0][0] == (card_id, "frozen")
    assert calls[1][0] == (card_id, "active")
